fixtures.prompt: Implement from the approved spec and Figma handoff, keep docs current, and verify against the source artifacts.
We have a client spec, but a few requirement gaps still need clearing up before planning.
We already have the PRD and need a phased implementation workflow with docs and verification.
The design and written requirements disagree on onboarding fields, so normalize the sources before coding.
Resume from the plan in /docs, keep blockers current, and execute the next slice.
The roadmap already exists; keep progress, verification, and deferred work honest while we keep building.
The project is too broad; break it into safe ordered slices with clear dependencies.
We know roughly what to build, but the next executable slice and safe parallel boundaries are unclear.
The docs are trustworthy enough; help me shape the next few slices and dependency order before execution continues.
There are stale progress docs and old exports everywhere; find the safest restart point first.
I inherited half-finished plans and verification notes; tell me what artifacts still deserve trust.
Before we resume, audit which of these trackers and handoff notes are still trustworthy enough to rely on.
Write a handoff for the next agent so they can continue this session without rediscovering context.
Compact this conversation into a continuation note with suggested skills, touched files, blockers, and the next step.
Before making a handoff, audit the stale plans and old exports to find which artifacts are still trustworthy.

clusters.name: skill package vs portfolio vs packaging

fixtures.prompt: Fix this skill package and validate the metadata, references, and prompt wiring.
Update this skill without letting the frontmatter, references, or handoffs drift.
Mine recent conversations for reusable conventions and update the editable skills without leaking project-specific memory.
The library feels crowded and repetitive; tell me what to keep, merge, refresh, or retire.
Audit the skill set for overlap, bloat, and stale triggers instead of just fixing one package.
Review the whole skill portfolio and tell me which owners should be refreshed or retired.
Scan the skill library after conversation-mined updates and flag overlap, bloat, or drift across mirrored roots.
Export the skills as a flattened self-contained bundle and include compatibility notes.
Generate the library catalog, export manifest, and universal bundle shape for downstream use.
I need the packaging and catalog outputs updated across Codex-native and flattened formats.

clusters.name: design polish vs redesign vs system export

fixtures.prompt: The screens exist but still do not match the target design's spacing, states, and assets.
We already built the routes; now make the UI match the handoff exactly and verify the states.
The product looks generic and under-designed; diagnose the biggest UI problems before we redesign.
Audit the current frontend first and tell me which redesign moves will have the highest impact.
Before redesigning, review the current UI and prioritize the biggest structural issues driving the weak feel.
The visual direction is stable now; turn it into reusable tokens and component rules.
We have settled on the product's visual language; export reusable design-system guidance for future implementation.
Capture the stable visual rules from the current product into reusable design-system artifacts.
We have a rough idea for a new user onboarding flow, but I want to compare a couple of product directions before we commit.
The redesign request is still fuzzy; shape the experience and write the design direction before we plan implementation.
The Figma is done; turn it into a PRD under docs so implementation planning can start.
Grill this implementation plan against the codebase, existing docs, and domain language before we build it.
Stress-test this plan against CONTEXT.md and ADRs, one question at a time.
Challenge this ticket plan before implementation and tell me where the terminology or tradeoffs are weak.
This spec mostly makes sense; ask the few high-impact questions that would change the implementation plan.
Read this Trello export including all comments and make a plan for the first five Sprint cards, plus Czech summaries for the card owner.
Plan the next Jira ticket batch, preserve board order, note prior AI changes, and give FE/BE contract notes.
From these Todo cards, tell me which are verification-only because another agent may already have implemented them.
The Trello evidence and fix plan are approved; implement the cards, verify each affected repo, and post concise closeout comments.
Resume this approved ticket batch from the active /docs plan and progress tracker, keeping blockers and verification evidence current.
Implement this approved Jira batch, verify the user-visible behavior, and write the stakeholder ticket comments afterward.
The ticket changes touch generated Flutter clients; regenerate them using repo tooling and report whether the outputs are tracked or ignored.
The ticket batch intake is approved; turn it into a traceable implementation workflow with docs and verification.
Audit this codebase for shallow modules and deepening opportunities before we refactor.
Find architecture seams where logic leaks across callers and suggest deep module candidates.
Before upgrading this framework, map migration risk, compatibility hazards, and rollout concerns.
Prototype this state machine so I can play with edge cases before we implement it.
Try three radically different UI variants for this dashboard on the existing route.
Explore product directions for this dashboard before deciding whether any prototype is needed.
Review this branch since main against the issue and repo standards.
Review WIP changes against AGENTS.md and the PRD, keeping standards and spec findings separate.
The review findings are fixed and verification evidence exists; decide if this is ready for release.
Make a detailed plan for another AI agent to test the Stripe Connect flow end to end in the browser.
Prepare an E2E verification handoff for uploads, roles, webhook state transitions, and mobile/web parity.
The implementation is done; prove the specific upload fix with fresh tests and browser evidence before saying it is fixed.
We have verification evidence now; decide if this feature is ready for handoff or release and list remaining risks.
Before coding, compare the frontend assumptions against the OpenAPI and live payload examples, read-only.
Review this backend response shape and tell me where the mobile app contract has drifted, but do not change code.
The API contract is confirmed; implement the frontend integration and verify the real browser flows.
This implementation is large enough for a lead-worker setup; keep planning and integration central but delegate bounded slices.
We have three disjoint write scopes and a clear plan; coordinate worker agents safely and keep final verification on the lead path.
Break the broad work into ordered slices first; we are not ready to delegate workers yet.
